title: OSCORE compute_nonce silently drops sender_id bytes >7
description:
# Problem
In `rust/lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs:576-579`:

```rust
// Bytes 1-7: left-padded sender ID (max 7 bytes)
if sender_id.len() <= 7 {
    let start = 8 - sender_id.len(); // bytes 1-7
    nonce[start..start + sender_id.len()].copy_from_slice(sender_id);
}
```

If `sender_id.len() > 7`, the ID is silently ignored. RFC 8613 allows IDs up to 8 bytes (`ID_MAX_LEN = 8`), but only 7 bytes fit in the nonce structure.

## What goes wrong
- A sender_id of 8 bytes (valid per `ID_MAX_LEN`) produces a nonce that ignores the ID entirely
- Two contexts with different 8-byte IDs would produce identical nonces
- This leads to nonce reuse and cryptographic failure

## Trigger
`Context::new(..., sender_id: &[0u8; 8], ...)` - valid input, corrupted output.

## Fix
Option 1: Validate sender_id.len() <= 7 in `Context::new` and return error
Option 2: Change ID_MAX_LEN to 7 to match actual nonce capacity
Option 3: Panic/error in `compute_nonce` if sender_id.len() > 7

Note: The caller `Context::new` does check `sender_id.len() <= ID_MAX_LEN` (8), but 8 > 7.
